purpose: Treat morphological resolution as a hidden-state machine: the OBSERVED surface (with whatever diacritics the source carries) is the emission; the CORRECT root/lemma/pos/form is the hidden state. norm() over-collapses emissions, so several distinct states map to one observation. Each case fixes the right hidden state, lists the plausible distractors a surface-keyed resolver would fall into, names the evidence that disambiguates, and the gate that decision must clear. Mirrors the regression style of ../examples/qamus-regressions.jsonl but adds the explicit distractor set + evidence the resolver must demand.

observation.norm_key: the over-collapsed key the resolver actually sees (no harakāt/hamza-seat/shadda)

correct_hidden_state.root: space-separated radicals or null for rootless particles
correct_hidden_state.pos: verb | noun | masdar | participle | adjective | particle | proper_noun | pronoun

case_schema.distractor_states: the hidden states a surface/norm-keyed resolver would wrongly emit, each with why it loses
case_schema.evidence_required: signals that must be present to certify the correct state; subset of qac | norm_strict | content_letter_harakah | hamza_seat | shadda | voice_pattern | wazn | context_nahw | referent

gate_legend.auto_safe: key collision-free AND single applicable sense — a form-aware gloss may ship cited
gate_legend.two_vote_required: derived-form sense shift / passive / negation-governed — two independent agreements needed
gate_legend.human_source_review_required: needs a photographed-source or context check beyond automatic signals
gate_legend.never_auto_resolve: the distinguishing signal is dropped by the key (or absent in source) — pending, never the commoner reading

evidence_sources_internal: qac/norm_strict/diacritic helpers are INTERNAL evidence only; the public record is always {src:'qamus',kind:'authored',lang:'en'} per ../../qamus/schemas/authored-gloss.schema.json

distractor_states.why_loses: treats the alif as a radical; the middle radical is و (hollow verb), surfacing as alif only in the perfect
a single-sense filter over-pends a high-frequency unambiguous verb
final-weak root is ي not و; the imperfect ـضِي preserves the ي radical
قَاضِي 'judge' shares the radicals but is a participle, not this finite imperfect
the imperfect drops the initial و (assimilated/miṯāl verb); norm sees only ج-د and reaches for a doubled root
wrong hollow root; the citation form وَجَدَ recovers the dropped و radical
the madda آ is hamza+alif of Form IV; Form I أَمِنَ is a different sense ('was secure')
norm collapses the hamza seat onto أيمان 'oaths'; norm_strict keeps the seat
norm drops the medial hamza, collapsing onto hollow سَالَ 'flowed'; the hamza seat is the only signal
different hollow root entirely; سَوَّلَ is Form II of س و ل
a two-letter key tempts a rootless reading; the shadda encodes the doubled (geminate) third radial
unrelated assimilated root; norm without the shadda has nothing to separate them
the شَدّة on the zāy marks Form II; its absence means Form I 'descended'
Form IV carries an initial hamza this surface does not
shares the norm key; only the shadda on the middle radical certifies Form II's causative/intensive sense
Form I is intransitive 'descended'; Form IV is transitive 'sent down'. The hamza+sukūn wazn (أَفْعَلَ) distinguishes
no such nominal here; the fatḥa pattern is verbal
the prefix here is ٱفْتَعَلَ (tāʾ infix), not ٱسْتَفْعَلَ; only one س, the tāʾ sits between
Form VIII adds 'listened attentively'; the wazn's tāʾ infix changes the sense register
Form X ٱسْتَفْعَلَ is 'sought forgiveness', a requestive sense Form I lacks
the fatḥa on the fāʾ marks a perfect, not the kasra imperative
the ḍamma-kasra vowel pattern marks the passive 'was created'; norm drops voice entirely
the dominant key reading (18x) is Form II active; the rare passive (1x) drops both shadda and voice, so the key cannot reach it
Form I active is a third reading the bare key cannot separate
Form IV imperfect يُخْرِجُ has ḍamma-then-kasra; Form I يَخْرُجُ has fatḥa-then-ḍamma. norm erases the vowel contrast that separates 'goes out' from 'brings out'
fatḥa pattern is the finite verb; the kasra-sukūn maṣdar takes a nominal gloss 'remembrance'
fatḥa-fatḥa is the concrete noun 'male'; three readings share the key, separable only by harakāt
norm collapses the initial hamza-kasra seat onto أيمان; the ي here is a glide, not a radical
this is the maṣdar of آمَنَ, a nominal, not a finite verb
the وَاو-نُون is the masculine sound plural of an ism fāʿil, not a verbal subject ending; ism fāʿil is nominal
the hamza-on-wāw seat (ؤ) is a Form IV participle marker, not a radical و
a verb-on-noun error; رَسُول is the فَعُول-pattern agent noun 'messenger'
the final ـًا is accusative tanwīn-alif, not the pronoun نا
fatḥa pattern would be the verb; the ḍamma-ḍamma فُعُل pattern is a broken plural
the broken plural has no surface affinity to its singular كِتَاب — must match by lemma/plural field, not surface
fatḥa-fatḥa is 'angel'; the ḍamma-sukūn مُلْك is 'dominion/sovereignty' — same root, vowel-split
fatḥa-kasra is 'king'; a triple homograph on one key, decided by vowels + context only
a data-error homograph filed عَلِيم under أ ل م; the ع-initial فَعِيل from ع ل م is 'All-Knowing', not the hamza-initial 'painful'
shares radicals with حَمِدَ but is a personal name (61:6); a name is not its root verb
the elative pattern exists but the referent here is a proper noun
the active participle 'righteous (deed)' is the default; the proper-noun reading needs context certification
kasra on the mīm would make it the preposition; مَن (no kasra, no shadda) is the relative/interrogative pronoun
a shadda on the nūn would make it the verb 'to bestow'; rootless particle has no content root
norm yields a لين-shaped key from a preposition + 1pl enclitic; there is no triliteral stem to certify
the final ـًا is accusative tanwīn-alef, not the pronoun نا; do not decompose
